paragraphs: The Ming Tombs
Intro: The Ming Tombs

Dear friends, the area we're entering now—surrounded by Tianshou Mountain and shaded by dense pines and cypresses—is the Ming Tombs in Beijing, the eternal resting place of 13 emperors from the Ming Dynasty. I'm your tour guide, Miya, and it's my great honor to be your companion today. We will follow the classic tour route: "Divine Way → Changling Tomb → Dingling Tomb." Let's set off right now!
各位朋友，咱们现在走进的这片被天寿山环抱、松柏掩映的区域，就是明朝13位皇帝的长眠之地——北京明十三陵！咱们顺着"神道→长陵→定陵"的经典路线逛。咱们这就出发！
I. Overview & Historical Evolution
1. Location & Layout

The Ming Tombs are located at the southern foot of Tianshou Mountain in Changping District, covering about 120 square kilometers. The layout follows the ancient Chinese "feng shui treasure land" principle: backing onto Tianshou Mountain, facing a gentle river, 13 mausoleums distributed around Changling Tomb like stars surrounding the moon. The entire cemetery is led by the Divine Way, lined with stone figures and stone animals.
明十三陵位于北京昌平区天寿山南麓，占地约120平方公里，整体布局遵循"风水宝地"原则：背靠天寿山，前临蜿蜒小河，13座陵寝以长陵为中心环绕分布。整个陵区以神道为先导，两旁排列着石人石兽。
2. Names of the 13 Mausoleums

Sorted chronologically: Changling (Yongle), Xianling (Hongxi), Jingling (Xuande), Yuling (Zhengtong/Tianshun), Maoling (Chenghua), Tailing (Hongzhi), Kangling (Zhengde), Yongling (Jiajing), Zhaoling (Longqing), Dingling (Wanli), Qingling (Taichang), Deling (Tianqi), Siling (Chongzhen). Changling is the largest; Dingling is the only one excavated and open to the public.
按时间排序：长陵（永乐）、献陵（洪熙）、景陵（宣德）、裕陵（正统/天顺）、茂陵（成化）、泰陵（弘治）、康陵（正德）、永陵（嘉靖）、昭陵（隆庆）、定陵（万历）、庆陵（泰昌）、德陵（天启）、思陵（崇祯）。长陵规模最大，定陵唯一发掘开放。
3. Ming Imperial Mausoleum Norms

All Ming imperial mausoleums follow a unified "square front and round back" layout. The front half is a square sacrificial area; the rear half is a round burial area called the Treasure City. This symbolizes "heaven is round and earth is square."
明代帝陵都遵循"前方后圆"布局：前半部分为方形祭祀区，后半部分为圆形安葬区宝城。象征"天圆地方"。
4. Emperors Not Buried in the 13 Mausoleums

Three emperors were not buried here: Zhu Yuanzhang was buried in Nanjing's Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum; Emperor Jianwen disappeared after the Jingnan Campaign; Emperor Chongzhen was buried in Siling Tomb outside the core area.
三位皇帝未入葬：开国皇帝朱元璋葬于南京明孝陵；建文帝朱允炆下落不明；崇祯皇帝葬于十三陵核心区外的思陵。
5. Changling's Site Selection & Construction

Changling is the mausoleum of Emperor Yongle. He chose Tianshou Mountain for three reasons: excellent feng shui, the need of capital relocation, and highlighting imperial power. Construction started in 1409, using precious golden nanmu from Yunnan and Sichuan.
长陵是永乐皇帝的陵寝。选定天寿山有三大原因：风水绝佳、迁都需求、彰显皇权。1409年动工，选用云南、四川的金丝楠木。
II. Changling Tomb – The "Wooden Miracle"
1. Emperor Yongle's Life

Emperor Yongle was the third emperor of the Ming Dynasty, reigning for 22 years. He accomplished three milestone events: launching the Jingnan Campaign, relocating the capital to Beijing, and building Changling Tomb.
永乐皇帝在位22年，完成三件大事：发动靖难之役、迁都北京、修建长陵。
2. Central Axis Buildings
From outside to inside: Tomb Gate, Gate of Eminent Favor, Hall of Eminent Favor, Inner Red Gate, Ming Tower, Treasure City.
从外到内：陵门、祾恩门、祾恩殿、内红门、明楼、宝城。
3. Hall of Eminent Favor

This grand hall, 29 meters high with 9 bays in width, is the largest existing wooden structural hall in China. It has 60 huge golden nanmu pillars—the thickest one has a diameter of 1.17 meters. The entire hall is built with wooden mortise and tenon joints without a single nail.
祾恩殿高29米、面宽9间，是中国现存最大的木构大殿。殿内60根金丝楠木柱，最粗的直径1.17米。全靠木榫卯拼接，没用一根钉子。
III. Dingling Tomb – The "Underground Palace"
1. Tomb Owner & Ground Buildings

Dingling is the mausoleum of Emperor Wanli, who reigned for 48 years—the longest in the Ming Dynasty. Most ground buildings were damaged by wars; our key visit is the underground palace, 27 meters deep.
定陵是万历皇帝的陵寝，在位48年，明朝最久。地面建筑大部分损毁，重点参观地下27米深处的地宫。
2. Excavation Process

Excavation started in 1956. The team found the Diamond Wall, the thick wall sealing the underground palace. The underground palace had been sealed for over 200 years with severe hypoxia. It opened to the public in 1959. Due to lessons learned, the country later banned active excavation of imperial tombs.
1956年启动发掘，找到金刚墙。地宫封闭200多年，严重缺氧。1959年对外开放。因惨痛教训，国家后来规定不再主动发掘帝王陵寝。
3. Underground Palace Layout

The underground palace covers 1,195 square meters, divided into five halls: Front Hall (entrance), Middle Hall (three white marble thrones and ever-burning lamps), Rear Hall (main burial area with Emperor Wanli's coffin and 26 red-lacquered boxes of treasures), and two empty Side Halls.
地宫总面积1195平方米，分五殿：前殿（入口）、中殿（三座汉白玉宝座和长明灯）、后殿（万历皇帝棺椁及26个红漆木箱的珍宝）、左右配殿（空置）。
4. Burial with Jade in the Golden Well

In the center of the coffin platform is the "Golden Well," only 30 centimeters in diameter, containing Emperor Wanli's jade articles. This "Burial with Jade in the Golden Well" ritual symbolizes connecting with earth's vitality and eternal immortality.
棺床正中间的"金井"直径仅30厘米，内藏万历皇帝玉器。"金井玉葬"仪式寓意接地气、灵魂不朽。
IV. World Honor
In 2003, the Ming Tombs were inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, praised as "an outstanding representative of Ming imperial mausoleums."
2003年，明十三陵被列入《世界遗产名录》，获评"明朝皇家陵寝的杰出代表"。
Farewell Speech

Dear friends, our tour of the Ming Tombs has come to a perfect end. I hope today's explanation can help you remember the wooden structural miracle of Changling and the secrets of Dingling's underground palace. Wish you a pleasant journey, goodbye!
各位朋友，明十三陵之旅到这里就圆满结束啦！希望今天的讲解能让大家记住长陵的木构奇迹、定陵的地宫秘密。祝大家旅途愉快，再见！
